visible.story: The Green Line's northern branch has been logging odd door faults since the cold snap began three weeks ago. Trains arrive on time, but at four elevated outdoor stations the doors stall partway through opening, then re-cycle, padding dwell times by ten to forty seconds. Riders complain; on-time performance at the terminus drifts. Maintenance has swapped door motors on two cars with no improvement. The fleet is mixed-age, but faults cluster on specific platforms rather than specific cars. The northern stations sit on a viaduct exposed to lake wind, and their platform-edge heater troughs—narrow channels along the gap between car and platform, meant to keep that strip clear—have been running on the old winter schedule. A track inspector mentioned in passing that the drip line from the canopy gutters had been redirected last autumn to spill closer to the platform edge. Overnight low temperatures have stayed below freezing, and morning sun does not reach the north-facing platforms until late. The control center sees elevated door obstruction sensor counts and increased motor current spikes during the open phase, but the obstruction beam itself reports clean. Underground stations on the same line show none of these symptoms. Operators have started manually re-cycling doors, which mostly works but slows everything down.
visible.question: Do not give a black-box prediction. Use the available records and targeted tests to explain what hidden story-plausible cause best accounts for the outcome pattern. Your answer should name the unobserved cause in ordinary language, give evidence from queried data, rule out plausible alternatives, state a decisive test, and recommend the next action (including its dose/level where applicable). The action names are intentionally neutral and do NOT reveal which one targets the cause; decide that from the data you collect, not from the labels.

observed_variables.name: OvernightAirChill
observed_variables.description: how cold the overnight air was at the station

observed_variables.name: CanopyDripVolume
observed_variables.description: measured runoff from canopy drainage near the platform edge

scale.higher: more drip water reaching the edge

observed_variables.name: WindExposureIndex
observed_variables.description: wind exposure at the platform from lake-side gusts

observed_variables.name: EdgeChannelServiceLag
observed_variables.description: days since the platform-edge channel was last inspected and cleared

observed_variables.name: DoorSillClearanceDelta
observed_variables.description: deviation of measured door-sill clearance from spec at berth

scale.higher: more intrusion into the sill clearance envelope

observed_variables.name: OpenPhaseMotorCurrent
observed_variables.description: peak motor current during the door open phase

observed_variables.name: OutdoorPlatformShare
observed_variables.description: fraction of recent stops at exposed outdoor platforms

observed_variables.name: TerminusOnTimeDrift
observed_variables.description: minutes of accumulated lateness at the terminus

observed_variables.name: DoorDwellOverrun
observed_variables.description: seconds of dwell beyond scheduled door cycle

observed_variables.name: DoorMotorAgeIndex
observed_variables.description: average age and wear of door motors on the consist

observed_variables.name: ObstructionBeamActivity
observed_variables.description: rate of obstruction beam triggers from rider belongings

observed_variables.name: ControlSoftwareBuildVariance
observed_variables.description: how far the door controller firmware deviates from latest baseline

observed_variables.name: InfraredEdgeScanReturn
observed_variables.description: return signal from a thermal scan of the platform edge

scale.higher: colder, more reflective ice-like surface detected

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenA
intervenable_variables.description: applies a graded thermal/clearing treatment along the platform-edge channel

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenB
intervenable_variables.description: reroutes canopy drainage away from the platform edge by varying degrees

intervenable_variables.name: ProtocolX
intervenable_variables.description: deploys a handheld thermal scanner sweep along the platform edge before first train

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenC
intervenable_variables.description: door motor replacement and lubrication at varying intensities

intervenable_variables.name: DwellSetpointDial
intervenable_variables.description: raises scheduled dwell time allowance at affected stations

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenD
intervenable_variables.description: skip-stop or single-door operation at affected platforms at varying levels

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenE
intervenable_variables.description: firmware rollback/patch cycle of varying scope on door controllers

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenF
intervenable_variables.description: adds station agents to assist boarding at varying staffing levels

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenG
intervenable_variables.description: repaints platform edge markings and refreshes signage

public_action_space.note: Each interventional query may set any subset of public actions up to the cap. Actions are non-binary: most take an ordered dose level and some take a continuous setpoint, so choosing the right level is part of the task. Action names are neutral and do not indicate which knob addresses the hidden cause.

discovery_protocol.task_goal: Infer a story-plausible hidden cause, not just an observed predictor.
discovery_protocol.important_warning: Observed measurements may be clues, downstream symptoms, or competing alternatives; none of them is automatically the hidden cause. Action names are neutral and do not tell you which knob targets the cause, and the best level is not always the maximum.

hidden_cause.name: PlatformGapIceAccretion
hidden_cause.plain_name: ice buildup in the platform-edge heater channel

actions.name: RegimenA
actions.description: applies a graded thermal/clearing treatment along the platform-edge channel

actions.name: RegimenB
actions.description: reroutes canopy drainage away from the platform edge by varying degrees

actions.name: ProtocolX
actions.description: deploys a handheld thermal scanner sweep along the platform edge before first train

actions.name: RegimenC
actions.description: door motor replacement and lubrication at varying intensities

actions.name: DwellSetpointDial
actions.description: raises scheduled dwell time allowance at affected stations

actions.name: RegimenD
actions.description: skip-stop or single-door operation at affected platforms at varying levels

actions.name: RegimenE
actions.description: firmware rollback/patch cycle of varying scope on door controllers

actions.name: RegimenF
actions.description: adds station agents to assist boarding at varying staffing levels

actions.name: RegimenG
actions.description: repaints platform edge markings and refreshes signage

gold_explanation.description: Subfreezing overnight temperatures combined with redirected canopy drip water freeze into the platform-edge heater channel, narrowing the door-sill clearance and causing doors to bind, draw extra current, and re-cycle.
evidence: Faults occur only at exposed outdoor platforms after cold nights and correlate with high CanopyDripVolume and OvernightAirChill, while underground stations are unaffected.
DoorSillClearanceDelta and OpenPhaseMotorCurrent rise together at affected berths, indicating physical interference rather than electronic faults.
ObstructionBeamActivity stays low and faults persist after motor swaps, pointing to something in the gap rather than in the car.
alternatives_ruled_out: Motor wear is unlikely because faults cluster by platform, not by car or DoorMotorAgeIndex, and motor swaps did not help.
Obstruction beam miscalibration is inconsistent with clean beam reports and low ObstructionBeamActivity.
Firmware drift is ruled out because ControlSoftwareBuildVariance does not track the geographic and temperature-dependent fault pattern.
gold_explanation.decisive_test: Run ProtocolX (pre-service infrared sweep of the platform edge) on cold mornings; a strong cold-surface return localized to the edge channel confirms ice accretion as the cause.
gold_explanation.why_action: A standard-dose RegimenA to clear and reheat the edge channel, paired with a moderate RegimenB to redirect canopy drainage, removes the ice and prevents recurrence without overheating or unnecessary service disruption.

latent_variables.PlatformGapIceAccretion: Continuous hidden state for ice buildup in the platform-edge heater channel; never directly visible to the scientist.
